We're going to draw onto the principles we've covered in module three, which is copywriting, but we're going to go much deeper into both the psychology and the technology. Email marketing is an art form. If you get this part right, you'll be able to increase your sales by over 100% with a flick of a switch. This module is really powerful, so as always, take your notes, get out your workbook, and I'll see you again on the other side. Welcome to our email marketing module. This one is exciting. Yup, a sales team working for you all day, every day, program to generate leads or make sales, and it's all on autopilot. So let's talk about the backbone of the S L M email marketing system. And this is it. This is our formula. It's our proprietary email marketing formula. It's how we do everything. Now, you guys so far, just as a refresher, are familiar with sales funnels. You understand it won't be purpose of a sales funnel right to extract the most amount of value from the customer. We can spend more money than our competitors and acquiring a customer thereby growing it the rate we want to grow and taking as much market share as we want. Now, the first page of that sales funnel is of course the opt-in page. We're going to send traffic to the opt-in page, which is the first page of our sales funnel. Now, that's where we are here. So we're going to be sending traffic to an opt-in page. And then once we've sent that traffic and that prospect has effectively converted, if you will remember what we did, what we would do at that point in time, is we send at the or we make the prospect another offer at the point of sales. We make them an immediate upsell right in this case, it's our SLO or our self liquidating offer. And if they purchase that and made another offer, which is our core product offer, right? We excuse me may or may not decide to have a series of upsells and downsells and then ultimately leading to a thank you page. So what we're talking about in email marketing is what we do with the email address once they've entered their details on our opt-in page, given us their email address. Okay, so here's the process. The first thing we're going to do is we're going to get that email address and we're going to send them an email, which is what we call an indoctrination email. An indoctrination, I don't love the word. I mean, it's a common internet marketing buzz talk. Now they use the word very, very regularly. But what indoctrination is for our purposes is welcome. It's a welcome email, it's an introduction email. It lets the prospect at this point that know more about us, more about our company, more about our product. So three 10s email, it's an email focusing almost exclusively on the three 10s and introducing the prospect to your world, to your universe, to your business. Now, in that email in particular, we're not actually doing any kind of a sell. We're not selling anything. We're just wanting to solidify the three 10s. Okay, so we know that we need to accomplish those three 10s before we can move further down the straight line. So being mindful of that, we send an indoctrination email with the sole objective of really establishing the three 10s as best we can. Now, once they've gone past that indoctrination state, they'll be sent another email. This is as soon as they've opted in. All right, guys, so they've just opted in. They've landed on our opt-in page, on our lead page. They've given us their email address. And on the back end, through the email marketing software that we're going to be exploring in a moment, we're sending them an indoctrination email right away. So just opted in, okay? And they may have left our page entirely and not purchased the SLO. Now they've got an indoctrination email, selling us, selling our product, selling our company. And then, on the same day, right away, right after they get their indoctrination email, they're sent a straight line content email. Now, the straight line content email number one, it's actually specifically designed to further move them down the funnel. Okay, so we're going to be assuming for this particular case that a prospect has opted in to take advantage of our free product. Remember, we're offering some kind of a lead magnet or an opt-in bribe. It's a free product. They opted in. They've taken advantage of it. Now they've been sent an indoctrination, but they've left. Okay, so they haven't gone on further down our sales funnel. They haven't purchased that SLO, that self-liquidating offer, which is that low ticket product that we're going to be selling people or offering people so that we can convert prospects into customers and then we're going to sell them a core product and so forth. But in this case, this particular prospect just wanted our free thing, whatever it was, our free product, our opt-in bribe, and then they left. They didn't purchase anything after that. We're not going to let them go. Remember our funnel follow-up? This is one of the ways we follow up. We do so through email marketing. Now, what we're going to do is we're going to send them that indoctrination email because we're really confused right now, right? We don't know why they haven't purchased and the straight-line methodology will teach us that if somebody, anybody who is closeable can be closed if you're able to establish a perfect 10 out of 10 that they like your product, that they like and trust you and they like and trust your company. So we don't know which of those three we haven't satisfied yet, but we're going to do a shotgun approach and we're just going to email them an indoctrination email. It really does a great job in selling the three 10s to the prospect and just introducing them to us or introducing us, excuse me, to them and inviting them or welcoming them into our world or into our universe, right? Which is your subscriber list. And then we're going to be sending them another email right away. It's not going to take any time. Right away, we're going to be sending them another email that does a little bit more selling. Now, the objective of this particular email is to get them to take that next step to purchase that SLO with a self-liquidating offer. So they're going to make that purchase ideally through the content we write in our email one. Now, when we're actually talking about drafting email one or writing email one, there's going to be a link in that email and it's going to send them directly to that SLO page. We're trying to sell them something, right? We're not going to be sending them back to the opt-in page. They've already opted in. We're going to go through this in detail. I want to give you guys a bird's eye view right now of what's happening. So again, they've opted in through our sales funnel. They've gone through our indoctrination series because they left. They didn't purchase the SLOs. They didn't purchase our self-liquidating offers. Now we're going to work a little harder in selling our three terms, but we haven't made them any kind of offer yet. So we're going to try to create a really nice email and in that email or within the body of that email, there's going to be a link and that link is going to direct them to the sales page for our SLO, our self-liquidating offer. So it's our self-liquidating offer page of our funnel and they may not have bought yet. So they've been presented that one offer. They still haven't bought. So it would be two offers at this time, right? One point of sale. So it was that immediate offer after they just opted in. They said, no, now we've presented, now we've solved the three tens and we've made them another offer, offer number two, which is, which is accomplished through our content email number one. And now we're going to send them another email, which is subject, which is a straight line content email number two, which gets a little bit more aggressive. It talks a little bit about, it adds a little bit of scarcity, usually in terms of the, either the offer going away or the, or the price changing. So there's going to be a dramatic increase in the price or so forth. We try to add a little bit of scarcity. And then finally, that should do the job and should lead to sales. So there's the funnel map or the formula map, excuse me, opt in through our funnel, try to sell them. Then they left without purchasing our SLO. Now, back onto the indoctrination email. We said its main functional purpose was actually to further establish it three times and it is. It's the main purpose. The other purpose is expected to help us with deliverability. And what do I mean by that? Deliverability, guys, is basically as well as list hygiene. It is concerned with actual actually making sure that our emails land in our prospects. Box. Okay. Because you guys are going to have some kick ass emails. You have the template there. You've also gone through the copywriting module. You have all of Jordan's scripts that he drafted himself that you could easily convert into really kick ass emails and really kick ass sales. Let us you have an awesome, awesome sales funnel that you guys are. You understand the bigger picture of email marketing and business in general. You understand so much and the strategy is so great and we've covered heaps. Right. But if we have everything on point to a T, everything's perfect. Our strategy is great. Our copy is great and so persuasive. You know, our, our subject lines, our body copy. I mean, we just nailed it. All the technical automation stuff that we're going to go through in this module. All perfect. Everything's fantastic. But the email doesn't land in the inbox. What value is any of it? Right. It's of absolutely no value. We need to make sure our emails land in the inbox all the time. And ideally, that's not the only to avoid the spam folder. We don't want to come anywhere near the spam folder. It's not ideally if they're using Gmail, it ends up in their primary tab as opposed to the promotions tab. Now, here's how we're going to go about deliverability. Deliverability remains a, a kind of nebulous amorphous, confusing subject matter. Right. It's nobody really knows the answers. Okay. People have come in and they've suggested this is exactly what you do to get your emails in the inbox. And then a few, a few months and sometimes a few years later, the major ESPs will come back and say, no, that's, that's not the case at all. For example, we often thought that click through rate was a major factor in determining deliverability. So we, you would actually have an email. So you guys will be familiar with this. You will have received an email and within the email, there's a hyperlink, right? You can click that hyperlink and land up on a page. We use them a lot, right? We use the hyperlink to send people to pages in our sales funnel. And we always believe before the longest time, we believe that clicking on those links increased our deliverability score and told the email service providers, this is a good clean email that the actual subscriber wants. They want to receive that email. Recently, probably about six or eight months ago. The major ESPs have come back and said, no, we don't do it. We don't look at ESP. We don't look at click through rates at all because it's actually a breach of privacy. It's nothing factor in. It's not even a, a sub metric. It's micro metric. It's nothing. We don't look at it at all. It's, it doesn't form ours. It doesn't form any part of our assessments and determine whether or not your emails will end in the inbox. But here is what does rule 80 20 guys, right? We want to do the smallest amount of work that's going to lead to the greatest amount of results. And the single greatest factor or metric in determining whether or not your emails will end up in the email inbox is your open rates and your spam complaints. So you want people to open your emails and you want as few people as possible to market a spam and or unsubscribe. But unsubscribe rates, spam complaints, these are no knows. They're going to happen. No matter what it is you do, they're going to happen. You're going to get spam complaints and you're going to get unsubscribes. But our objective here is to build such a great relationship with that this that we limit or mitigate the amount of people who either unsubscribe from our list or for, or who click spam. You know, this is a spam message and the flag us is having sent them unsolicited emails. That's probably the biggest factor. And then the open rates guys, it's not that challenging, right? For why you get a great open rate is by having a great subject line. It's as simple as that. You have an awesome subject line. People are going to open the email. People are just going to open it. People are going to want to open your email if you have awesome, great subject lines. Now, the way we go about that is by going back to our copywriting course, taking a look at some of the copywriting strategies we spoke about before headlines. You know, headlines transfer really well to subject lines. You know, so you can think of a great headline that's short and punchy. You know, not those really long ones, but an awesome, nice short punchy headline. And that could make for a great email subject line. The other thing are open subject blind, blind subject lines or subject lines that are that are what we call cliffhangers, right? So people don't know what it is you're talking about unless they click the link. Often a cliffhanger subject line will end with an ellipse, right? Or dot, dot, dot, something like I can't believe this dot, dot, dot. You almost have to open it to find out what it is that this person can't believe. You know, so that's going to get a high open rate. I mean, don't use black hat tricks. I don't like tricks. I like sound marketing principles. But that being said, you can absolutely take advantage of strategies like cliffhangers or open loops. You know, open loops. People just want to close those loops. So use your creativity. Put your thinking caps on. Go through your copywriting exercise that you had done in the copywriting module and think of some great subject lines. Even look at your own inbox and look at the messages, not the ones that are coming from personal people. So no friends, no family, no colleagues. But look at the email, the emails that have been sent by different companies that you felt compelled to open, that you had to open. You can ask yourself, why is it that I opened it? Now, what was so compelling about it? Was it that it was curiosity based? No need to satisfy that curiosity. Was it so benefit driven? They made a huge promise and you just needed to find out what it was. Was there some kind of scarcity attached? Like, was there a final chance we're closing now or it's almost over or something like that? They really got you thinking and really encourage you to click that email to have a look at what was written in the email body copy. Think about that because remember, guys, your open rate is one of the largest factors or metrics in determining your delivery. Your availability as is your spam complaints. So we want to minimize or mitigate our spam complaints and mitigate our unsubscribes. Now, here's a strategy that we're going to talk about with list hygiene. So list hygiene, of course, is just cleaning up the list. So making sure that we have a great list and that list is responsive and they're opening up our emails. Even if they're not buying, that's fine because remember, click through rate is not a metric. So you can send emails to people. They've never made a purchase. That's fine. Never click on the link to go onto your sales funnel page, the relevant sales funnel page. That's fine. No problem as long as they're opening it. But if they're not opening it at all, we're in trouble. And this is how you manage that process. As your list grows, the larger it gets now, I've managed some huge list, enormous list. And the larger the list goes, the more people who are not going to be opening it, playing the numbers will tell you just that, right? You'll know that yourself, if you have a really large list, you know, lists of, say, more than 100,000, 100,000, 500,000 people on that list, then you're going to have a huge number of people who are not opening that list. And that is an absolute deliverability nightmare. Very hard to get through to the inbox. Even your hyper responsive prospects or hyper responsive subscribers aren't seeing your emails often because the others are holding them back because you're being flagged. So what I want you guys to do is just be mindful of this little trick, this hack. If somebody hasn't opened your email in 30, ideally 30, but if you've got a really small list and you're not going to lose any subscribers or take them off that list, maximum 60. So between 30 and 60 days, they haven't opened an email. Get rid of them. You delete them from your list. You take them out because they can only hurt you. Now, I'm not saying that we're just going to forfeit them entirely. That's not the case at all, but we're going to take them out and put them into another order responder solution and we're just going to send them broadcast emails and make them off as, okay, so you're going to pull out all these guys who are unresponsive, click them all together, go to another order responder service and we're going to be talking about the different tools we use. So you can't use your same service. It's not that you can create another campaign and just say, okay, this is my bad campaign. This is my good campaign. It doesn't work that way. We need to take them off of that order responder platform entirely, put them onto a new order responder platform and in this case, we're just going to be sending them timed broadcast emails. So we're going to be sending them an email saying we might have a sale or we may have an offer or a new product coming out or whatever. We're just going to blast that whole list with a broadcast email. But you're going to have a core list, right? This is your hyper responsive list. Treat it like gold. It is worth so much to you. That's a promise. It will change the way you do business forever if you treat your list well and you develop a good, strong relationship with them and they like and trust you and they also love all the products you have to offer them. Tool time. So now the tools here are not that challenging. I especially selected tools that were going to be easy so I can walk you guys through the process. You guys will just, just bear with me as we run through the tools. The only two really important tools, one of them you're already familiar with, which is our funnel page software because we have to go back into that to do integrations, right? And the other one is our email solution software or email marketing software. So let's take a look. When we're talking about email marketing software, what we're talking about is email marketing order responders. What an order responder is. It is a piece of software that sends out emails the way you dictate. Okay, so you can automate emails any way you like and it will send them out. So you can send them based on time, like two days after somebody subscribes, send them these three days after they subscribe, send them that. You can do it based on behavioral actions. For example, if somebody's clicked this particular link, send them this email. If they haven't clicked that link, don't send them that email. And the options are almost limitless and we're going to go through them. But you have a huge selection to choose from. You know, you have three options like MailChimp. They have a small free option and you can upgrade to a ProL account. You have an active campaign has some awesome automation. You can do some wild things in active campaign that you can't do in the others. You've got constant contact. You've got Aweber. Aweber is the only one that I'm putting in asterisks next to because while it's an awesome solution, it was one of the first solutions. And since it was one of the first solutions, a lot of spammers jumped on Aweber because they all wanted to send out mass emails. And as a result, Aweber kind of has gotten flagged by some of the major email service providers like Gmail and Outlook and the like. So Yahoo and so forth. So their deliverability might be as strong as the others. Okay, but I'm not saying anything bad about Aweber. If you're using Aweber now, that's fine. I'm just saying if you're going to start brand new, then maybe that's not one I would consider. And then you've got one of my favorites and a really great easy one to use, which is GetResponse. And because it's so easy to use, I'm going to use it as we do a walkthrough. Right? So this is GetResponse here. And it is a great piece of software. It is completely, it allows for some massive automation and it's so easy to use. And by the end of this module, you guys are going to be flying. Let's go into GetResponse right away. Okay, we'll just set up a basic email campaign, an email marketing campaign. I'll show you guys just how easy it is. And once we're done, then we'll integrate our email marketing campaign with our funnel building software, which is in this case, ClickFunnels. So we started with ClickFunnels in our funnel module. So I'm going to maintain that so we can just run on, so we can maintain that continuity. Excuse me. And then we're going to move on to ClickFunnels. We're going to integrate the two. So when somebody puts their email address on their opt-in page in ClickFunnels, it triggers all the work that's going to be done by our email order responder, which is GetResponse. And then we can start making money and running campaigns in our sleep. But before we get started, we have to work our way around GetResponse. So we can, we need to learn how to set up an email campaign, not just any email campaign, but a kick-ass high-converting email campaign that gets delivered to the inbox every time and makes enormous amount of sales by selling ourselves, selling our company, and selling our product in a really persuasive way. So let's get started on GetResponse. All right, guys, here we are at GetResponse. So I've just put in my username and password. And now this is the first page I see. For those of you who don't have an account, I think click GetResponse. We're offering a 30-day free trial. So take advantage of it, see whether or not you're happy with the system or you want to try another email responder software. But this is a really easy one to use, and it's pretty powerful. And deliverability is pretty good to give it a crack. All right, now you guys will know that it's unlikely you're going to have one sales funnel, right? Because you can have so many different diverse products and so many diverse product offerings that it doesn't make sense for you to just bolt on one product that has absolutely nothing to do with your core product and just bolt it on as an upsell or downsell or whatever to try to increase the value of the customer. Sometimes you're going to have to need to, you're going to need to, excuse me, create an entirely new funnel. And in that case, you're going to need to create an entirely new campaign in GetResponse. But for our purposes, we'll create a funnel, we'll create a campaign, and we'll call it a training campaign. So again, for every funnel you have, we're going to create a campaign. All that is going to create my campaign, drop down and click Create New Campaign. We're going to call this Practice, or have to be Law School, I think. Practice, SLM. All right, so let's create that campaign. All right, so the campaign's created. It's that easy. Now, if you have an existing email list, just click this Add Contacts button and manually add your contacts. So you'll just put the name of the contact and their email address and then just confirm that you have permission to send an email to them. So there's no spam complaint or no issues. GetResponse wants to know that you only send the emails that are solicited, no un-solicited emails. Now, if you have lots of emails, you don't want to actually do that all manually. So you just go to Import Contacts. You can copy and paste them here, or you can just upload a CSP file of all your email address or even an Excel file. Just get all your email addresses, upload them here, and then we put on this brand new list on our campaign, which is our Practice SLM campaign. All right, so now that we've set up a campaign and we've got the contacts in there, for those of you who are doing a brand new campaign that have no existing contacts, that contacts excuse me, that's fine, this is where every time somebody actually opts in, in our opt-in page, they're going to be put onto this particular list into this campaign and it can be empty now, it's fine. All right, so let's say Create Order Responders for Messages. Down at Create Order Responder. All right, so we can create an order responder to react based on a few different triggers. So it can be a time-based trigger. So day one send this, day three send that and so forth. It could be click-based. So if somebody's clicked this particular hyperlink, I want you to send them this email. If they don't click that link in the email body, I don't want you to send them that email. It can be based on opens, for example, if somebody's opened this particular email, send another follow-up email. If they haven't opened, leave them alone. They're just obviously not interested or don't want to send them any more emails. Now you can do it on goals. So goals is an interesting one because you would need to create a goal and then you would need to just get a snippet of code. So create a goal, create goal here. I'll leave this page. I get this code, I copy it. Right, I just copy this piece of code here and then I paste it into ClickFunnels. I'll show you where you need to paste it into ClickFunnels. And now GetResponse knows that every time somebody lands on that page with this particular code, send them this email. If they land on a page with another code, send them that email. So we've created a goal. And that's particularly helpful because you guys will notice if somebody purchases a particular product, let's look at this. Look at this analogy. Somebody's actually opted into our opt-in page, the first page of our funnel, and they haven't purchased our SLO. So what would happen is they would have landed on our opt-in page. We know that. We can put that code on the opt-in page. They would have landed on our SLO. We can put a new code on the SLO. But they would not have landed on the third page, right? We know they wouldn't have landed on the third page because if they landed on the third page, which is our core product page, it means they would have purchased our SLO product. So now we know, we can set a rule that if somebody's purchased our SLO products, send them this email. If they haven't purchased our SLO product, send them that email. That way we can tailor our email messages to encourage prospects or customers to move further down the sales funnel or to try to re-engage them into the sales funnel at whatever point it is that they dropped off. We know they didn't make it all the way to the end. You can use codes to manage that process by setting goals and get response. To make things nice and easy. All right. So we need to load a particular order respondent. This one looks loaded. All right. OK. I know. So we've got practice SLM. Here we are. Practice SLM. Time-based and we'll say day zero. If you're in, say, the insurance game or, you know, mortgages or any kind of business where your customer or client is going to want to speak to you and connect with you personally, then I encourage you to leave it. So I'd encourage you guys to do that and you can get rid of it like that. Couldn't be simpler. Go into the settings. Here's your Favocon. So basically, guys, you'll notice at the top of any page, any tab of a particular website, you're going to have a little logo. Quite often you have a logo and if you don't have your own logo, and that logo is called the Favocon and if you don't have your own logo, you're going to get the default click funnels logo, which is the click funnels logo and it may cause some brand incontinuity. It might not be really congruent with what you're presenting. You have another brand's logo there listed on your tab. So what you can do is you can put your own logo simply by inputting the URL of your logo here in the Favocon URL section. If you don't have your logo actually sitting up somewhere online, there's an easy way to go about this. You can simply just add a digital asset. So I'm going to digital assets. Then I'll add a new digital asset and that could be any kind of a logo, whatever you just save it on your computer and just upload it. It could be a logo and then you say create digital asset and eventually you'll be sent something like this. And there's going to be a URL here. You can just copy that URL and paste it into that section where it says Favocon URL and then your logo will appear in that little tab as opposed to click funnels logo. Oh no, that's right. Being able to communicate effectively with your prospects and customers through email is a mandatory weapon in your straight line marketing arsenal. By implementing what you've just been taught, you'll be able to take your business to the next level almost instantly. Remember, straight line marketing is all about sales and results.
